hydrofoil.title: Hydrofoil Flow-Control Analysis
what: Investigated the effect of passive vortex generators on the performance of a NACA 63-618 tidal turbine hydrofoil.
Conducted CFD simulations in ANSYS Fluent and validated results through wind tunnel testing.
Designed and tested multiple rectangular and triangular vortex generator configurations.
how: Modelled the hydrofoil and vortex generator configurations in SOLIDWORKS.
Performed CFD analysis using ANSYS Fluent with the SST k-ω turbulence model.
Manufactured test models using 3D printing and conducted wind tunnel experiments.
Analysed lift, drag, flow separation, and stall characteristics across different angles of attack.
Identified triangular vortex generators as the best-performing configuration, achieving up to 20% improvement in lift performance.
why: To understand how vortex generators can delay flow separation and improve hydrofoil performance.
To increase lift, reduce drag, and improve the efficiency of tidal turbine blades.
To compare computational predictions with experimental results and assess the effectiveness of different vortex generator designs.

conveyor-system.title: Belt Conveyor System Design
what: Designed and modelled a bottle twisting mechanism (Twister) in SOLIDWORKS to study product orientation in conveyor and packaging systems.
Created the geometry using multiple cross-sections and guide curves to generate a gradual 90° twisting path.
Performed motion analysis to understand how a bottle interacts with the guide structure while travelling through the system.
Assigned 1060 Aluminium Alloy to the twister and Acrylic to the bottle for material and contact analysis.
how: Created a series of sketches on offset planes representing the bottle profile at different rotational positions.
Generated the twisting guide structure by connecting the profiles and constructing the supporting framework.
Built the assembly in SOLIDWORKS and defined material properties for all components.
Conducted motion studies to analyse bottle movement through the twister and evaluate the effect of contact and friction.
why: To understand how products can be reoriented automatically without manual handling in industrial conveyor systems.
To gain practical experience in CAD modelling, motion simulation, material selection, and contact analysis.
To develop knowledge of the design considerations involved in packaging, manufacturing, and material handling equipment.
To explore how friction, contact forces, and guide geometry influence product movement and orientation.

laser-housing.title: Laser Housing Thermal Optimisation
what: Assessed steady-state thermal performance of a finned laser housing
Modelled conjugate heat transfer across water, helium, air and aluminium
Targeted 523 K helium with water and aluminium kept within limits
how: Built a quarter-symmetry CFD model in ANSYS Fluent
Ran a mesh independence study (under 1% variation)
Optimised inputs with ANSYS DesignXplorer (DOE + response surface)
why: Met every temperature target after optimisation
Cut required air velocity and water flow by about 50%
Showed simulation can size the cooling without overdesign
